entry.title: Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
In this blog post I’d like to outline the innovations for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 in the area of Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management. These are the topics that are covered: 

  • Staffing Resources in Enterprise Projects Using SAP Project and Resource Management (4RF*)  
  • End-to-End Project Cost Visibility: Enhancements in Project Cost Report (1NT*) 
  • Maintain WBS Element Milestones (1NT*) 

Check out my video to get a glimpse of the new features: 

The world of project management is constantly evolving, with new tools and software updates enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of project delivery. With the latest release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502, enterprise project managers and financial controllers are in for a treat. Two standout enhancements are set to revolutionize the way project costs and milestones are managed: the Project Cost Report enhancements and the ability to maintain milestones at the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element level. Moreover, we’re providing an integration with SAP Project and Resource Management which allows for the staffing of resources in enterprise projects using an external resource management solution.  

Let's dive into these features and explore their benefits for users. 

 

Staffing Resources in Enterprise Projects Using SAP Project and Resource Management  

In today's fast-paced business environment, efficient resource management is crucial for the success of enterprise projects. Utilizing an external resource management solution can significantly streamline the staffing process, ensuring that the right people with the right skills are assigned to the projects when needed. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition seamlessly integrates with SAP Project and Resource Management to simplify this task.  
 
Enterprise projects often have intricate staffing needs, requiring specific skills and timely availability. The integration between SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Project and Resource Management addresses these complexities by enabling a seamless flow of resource requests. Project managers can create detailed resource requests, specifying necessary skills, availability, and relevant project timelines. Once these requests are published, resource managers take over and match resources to requests based on skill fit and availability. Leveraging visualization and filtering options as well as the AI-assisted matching of employees to projects ensures optimal staffing for projects.  

Figure 1: The integration of SAP Project and Resource Management increases transparency regarding resource utilization.   

 
Benefits  
 
1. Enhanced Flexibility: This integration supports both direct and external staffing. While direct staffing is used for internal employees associated with the same cost center, external staffing allows for broader resource planning using external solutions, regardless of cost center allocations. 
 
2. Real-Time Updates: Upon a hard booking of a resource, all staffing information is updated in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in real-time. This ensures transparency and up-to-date workforce utilization, aiding managers in making informed decisions. 
 
3. Comprehensive Visibility: Utilizing the “Manage My Timesheet” application, employees can access tasks related to their assignments, streamlining time tracking and ensuring accurate project accounting. 
 
The integration with SAP Project and Resource Management can significantly optimize resource staffing for enterprise projects. By leveraging this integration, enterprises can achieve a higher level of efficiency and accuracy in managing their project teams, ultimately leading to successful project outcomes. For detailed configuration guidance, users should refer to the SAP Help Portal and specific user guides available for SAP Project and Resource Management. 

If you want to find out about more highlights in this area, please check out the latest blog post by my colleague Katharina Stopf.   

 

 

 
End-to-End Project Cost Visibility 

In large-scale enterprise projects, maintaining clear visibility over project costs is crucial. Traditionally, project cost reports primarily focused on commitment costs directly associated with the project's consumption. This left a gap in understanding the ongoing and real-time insights related to project stock commitment costs and inventory-related costs that were in project stock but not yet consumed. 

With the latest enhancements in the Project Cost Report, we’re closing the last gaps in achieving end-to-end project cost visibility. The inclusion of two new columns – one for commitments related to project stock and one for inventory costs – brings a more comprehensive picture to project managers and financial controllers.  

Take a look at the demo to find out more.  

Figure 2: Providing full visibility into all cost types throughout the project’s execution cycle.   

 
Benefits  

  1. Holistic View of Project Stock Commitment Costs: The updated report provides insights into commitment costs that are tied to project stock, allowing project managers to anticipate future expenses better and plan accordingly. 
  2. Visibility of Inventory-related Costs: Users can now track inventory costs for materials not yet issued to the project. This feature helps in forecasting project expenses and ensuring resources are adequately allocated where needed. 
  3. Aggregated and non-aggregated Views: Depending on the analysis requirements, users can opt for either a detailed or summary view of the costs, offering flexibility in reporting. 

Figure 3: Two new columns as part of the Project Cost Report allow for insights into project stock commitment costs and inventory-related costs.   

 
These enhancements offer users greater transparency and control over project finances. By having all relevant costs visible in a single report, financial discrepancies can be minimized, and more accurate and timely project-financial decisions can be made. This improvement ultimately aids in budgeting accuracy and effective financial resource management, aligning with an organization's strategic financial goals. 
 

 

 

Maintain WBS Element Milestones 

Tracking project progress via milestones is key in ensuring timely project completion. However, managing milestones solely at the project level can often mean missing out on the granularity and detailed tracking needed for larger projects that use multiple WBS elements. 
 

With the introduction of milestones at the WBS element level, users can now create, manage, and track milestones specific to smaller components of the project.  

Figure 4: When maintaining milestones at WBS element level, they can be flagged as “Main Milestone” if it’s essential for the project planning. 

Benefits

  1. Track Progress at a Granular Level: By creating milestones for each WBS element, project managers can monitor the progress of each project component more precisely. 
  2. Flexibility and Precision in Planning: Milestones in the WBS element allow for more precise scheduling and resource allocation, ensuring each project component meets its deadlines. 
  3. Enhanced Project Tracking and Reporting: With milestones now visible at finer levels, the project progress and status become clearer and can be reported with greater accuracy. 
     
    The ability to define milestones at the WBS level enhances the foresight and control project managers have over a project's lifecycle. By providing clear checkpoints at various stages of the project, potential issues can be identified and resolved earlier, ensuring projects stay on track. This feature is particularly beneficial for large, complex projects where detailed oversight is crucial for success. 
     

 
The latest enhancements in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management are set to revolutionize the way projects are managed. With more detailed insights into project costs and the ability to track progress at the WBS level, these features empower project managers and financial controllers with the tools they need to optimize project execution, ensure budget adherence, and achieve strategic project goals. With the integration of SAP Project and Resource Management, resource managers are equipped with optimal staffing requirements for enterprise projects providing a higher level of efficiency and accuracy in managing project teams 

 

 

Topics Under Discovery 

To improve the offerings we have at SAP, we have a program in place which looks at topics that were raised as part of the Customer Engagement Initiative. Here we currently have two under discovery for which our customers had the biggest interest and for which we’d like to get your feedback.  

  1. Automated Planned Costs and Revenues  

The first topic focuses on automated planned costs and revenues. Currently, users must manually upload costs and revenues using tools like SAC planning or Excel sheets into the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system. The aim is to enhance this process by deriving planned costs from logistics data and sales orders, enabling a streamlined automatic integration. 
 
     2. Settlement from Projects with Revenue​ 

The second topic of discovery involves the settlement of projects with revenue. Presently, only overhead and investment projects have settlement capabilities. However, there has been interest from clients to extend this functionality to revenue-generating projects as well. The exploration here involves understanding of customer requirements for both simple and complex settlements, including revenue recognition impacts.  

 

Please reach out if you want to receive more information as we aim to incorporate feedback from experienced project financial controllers and managers to steadily improve our offerings. 

 

 

 

The Future of Business, Today: Get Your Access to AI-Assisted Features Thanks to this Limited Promotion Now!  

The 2502 update is a strategic investment that combines advanced AI with powerful enterprise management tools. Whether scaling your business, enhancing productivity, or building sustainable operations, its capabilities help support a more agile and resilient future. 

Sign up for following features through the limited Promotion for AI-assisted Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (for all countries, customers, and partners): 

  • The easy filter and smart summarization features in SAP Fiori allow for efficient, natural language filtering of business data and seamless summarization capabilities, enabling users to quickly extract relevant information and jump-start subsequent collaboration.  
  • Facilitate easier understanding and interpretation of financial data with insights from cost center reviews and explanations of fixed-asset depreciation keys. 

 

 

More Information 

If you are interested in the latest and greatest blog posts on Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management, please have a look at my collection blog and also take a look at the Community page for Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management (Project Control). 

 

Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!    

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience – anywhere, anytime.

entry.title: Upcoming Webinar: The Future of AI for Talent Management
entry.content:
As the world of work continues to evolve, organizations must rethink how they attract, develop, and retain talent. With AI and talent intelligence playing an increasingly critical role, HR leaders need to embrace innovative strategies to stay ahead. Join us for an engaging webinar where we’ll explore how AI-driven innovations and skills-based talent strategies can help your organization unlock workforce potential and drive business success.

Join us on Tuesday, March 4th at 11:00 AM EST to discover how using an AI-enabled talent management system enables organizations to hire the best talent, create targeted learning pathways, optimize performance, and deliver personalized development opportunities at scale.

Key topics in this webinar include:

  • The latest HR trends and research insights on how AI is transforming talent management.
  • SAP’s AI strategy and how it supports accelerated growth, employee productivity, and skill development.
  • Live demo showcasing AI-driven innovations for attracting, retaining, and developing top talent.

Don’t miss this opportunity to see how AI can reshape your talent management strategy and empower your workforce to adapt, grow, and thrive.

Register now!

entry.title: Artificial Intelligence & Technology in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
Welcome to my blog post on Artificial Intelligence and Technologies, showcasing the groundbreaking innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Release 2502. In this release, we are thrilled to unveil a series of advanced AI-powered features that redefine how businesses interact with their enterprise systems. From Joule, our intelligent AI copilot, to AI-assisted financial insights, and natural language-driven functionalities, these capabilities are designed to elevate user productivity, streamline decision-making, and unlock new levels of operational efficiency. A heartfelt thank you to our talented developers and visionaries for their dedication to driving innovation forward!

Watch my video for an overview of the top new features:

 

 

1. Joule: The AI Copilot

Video: Create Customized Insight Card with Joule

Video: Search Open Sale Order with Joule

 

Joule is now generally available and seamlessly integrated in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition after being in early adoption program. SAP’s AI copilot Joule leverages generative AI to empower users to execute their everyday tasks more efficiently and use natural language to express their business need.

Value Proposition:

  • Enables quick navigation to applications for seamless task execution.
  • Delivers instant insights into critical business data, such as purchase orders.
  • Provides concise summaries of user enablement content for faster task completion.
  • Simplifies personalization of the "My Home" page, improving user productivity.

Capabilities:

  • Recommends relevant applications to users and facilitates seamless navigation.
  • Offers insights about business objects and situations without requiring users to open separate applications.
  • Summarizes help content to answer queries expressed in natural language.
  • Allows users to create preview cards directly in Joule and add them to the "My Home" Insights section.

For more information, see: Joule Capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

Joule for Situation Handling

The integration of SAP's AI copilot Joule with Situation Handling enables users to receive up-to-date information on demand. Joule displays relevant issues, shares additional information, and directs users to the appropriate apps, ensuring a continuous overview of responsibilities while working on their daily tasks.

Value Proposition:

  • Offers intelligent assistance for rapid decision-making and issue resolution.
  • Proactively identifies and addresses issues to avoid operational disruptions or financial risks.

Capabilities:

  • Ask Joule to identify situations requiring attention and provides contextual information.
  • Proposes solutions and directs users to related applications for streamlined resolution.

 

2. General Availability of AI-assisted Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

After successful early adopter care programs, we are pleased to announce the general availability of the following AI-assisted features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Release 2502. The 2502 update is a strategic investment that combines advanced AI with powerful enterprise management tools. Whether scaling your business, enhancing productivity, or building sustainable operations, its capabilities help support a more agile and resilient future.

AI-Assisted Smart Summarization

AI-assisted smart summarization allows you to summarize object pages of SAP Fiori elements-based applications with the help of the power of generative AI.

The summarization can be reviewed and modified in a rich text editor and easily copied into productivity tools, such as email or chat, for follow-ups with co-workers and customers.

Value Proposition:

  • Better understanding of business data
  • Time saving when preparing business data for follow ups
  • Trustworthy AI results
  • Easy editing
  • Efficient sharing

Capabilities:

  • Selection of sections to be summarized
  • Well-structured output
  • Rich text editor for review and edits
  • Copy to clipboard for follow-ups
  • Possibility to leave sentiment of the feature

 

AI-Assisted Easy Filter

AI-assisted easy filter allows you to filter list reports of SAP Fiori elements-based applications through natural language with the help of the power of generative AI.

Instead of manually setting the filter values through type ahead and value helps, you type your search query into the filter field, and you can easily access recent filter queries and favorite queries as well.

Value Proposition:

  • Better understanding of business data
  • Time saving when filtering based on known filter criteria
  • Trustworthy AI results
  • Intuitive, click-efficient user experience, especially for new users

Capabilities:

  • Filtering based on natural language
  • Support of filter fields that are available, but not visible in the filter bar
  • Complex filters like amount and currency, date ranges, etc. are supported
  • Adjustment of the filters in the filter tokens
  • Access to recent filter queries
  • Support to mark recent filters as favorites

 

AI-Assisted Financial Business Insights in Finance

The "Virtual Analyst" within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition transforms financial data analysis by providing immediate explanations and summarizations of insights in natural language. This AI feature allows users to quickly discover what’s meaningful within selected data in Review Booklets, analyzing extensive data sets, highlighting dependencies, and proposing actions. Benefits include:

  • Immediate Insights: Quick explanations and summarizations of data insights in natural language.
  • Comprehensive Analysis: Analyzes extensive data sets, highlights dependencies, and proposes actions.
  • Intuitive Visualizations: Presents findings in visual, tabular, and plain language formats, aiding management reporting and decision-making.

 

AI-Assisted Error Resolution (SAP Advanced Financial Closing)

AI-Assisted Error Resolution in SAP S/4HANA Cloud offers a comprehensive solution to common error situations. It generates understandable explanations of technical error messages, providing end-users with detailed resolution steps. AI-Assisted Error Resolution in SAP S/4HANA Cloud offers:

  •   Comprehensible Explanations: Generates understandable explanations of technical error messages, making complex issues more accessible to end-users.
  •   Step-by-Step Guides: Provides detailed resolution steps to help users solve errors independently.
  •   Time Savings: Expedites error resolution during critical financial processes, reducing delays and operational pressure. This feature helps end-users resolve common errors independently, improving operational efficiency and reducing reliance on SAP support.

This feature significantly enhances the efficiency and effectiveness of financial reporting and decision-making processes.

 

Form Extension in Localization as a Self-Service

Form Extension in Localization as a Self-Service simplifies and accelerates the process of generating and updating customized forms within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This feature enables businesses to automatically create localized form templates based on specific requirements, bind relevant data sources, and seamlessly upload them into the system. By automating these traditionally manual tasks, organizations can significantly reduce the time and cost associated with form localization—by up to 95%. This enhancement is particularly valuable for companies operating in multiple regions, ensuring compliance with local regulations while maintaining operational efficiency. Designed as a cross-industry solution, this capability empowers businesses to adapt quickly to evolving documentation needs without the complexity of custom development.

More information: Link

 

The Future of Business, Today: Get Your Access to AI-Assisted Features Thanks to this Limited Promotion Now! 

Sign up for following features through the limited Promotion for AI-assisted Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (for all countries, customers, and partners):

  • The easy filter and smart summarization features in SAP Fiori allow for efficient, natural language filtering of business data and seamless summarization capabilities, enabling users to quickly extract relevant information and jump-start subsequent collaboration. 
  • Facilitate easier understanding and interpretation of financial data with insights from cost center reviews and explanations of fixed-asset depreciation keys.

 

3. Beta Programs of New AI-assisted Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Financial Business Insights for Group Financial Statements (Beta)

This feature, currently in beta, introduces cutting-edge capabilities to simplify complex financial reporting. By integrating AI, it helps businesses uncover insights and improve collaboration.

Value Proposition:

  • Supports strategic decisions by explaining complex financial KPIs and merging insights with user comments.
  • Streamlines financial reporting with contextualized summaries of critical data.
  • Enhances collaboration and clarity in financial reviews.

Capabilities:

  • Leverages generative AI to summarize data sets and calculate context-relevant KPIs.
  • Combines data with user comments for richer summaries.
  • Provides filtering and navigation tools based on business insights.

 

Generative AI Recommendations for Situation Handling (Beta)

This feature leverages generative AI to provide context-specific recommendations, tailored to streamline decision-making with targeted recommendations for Situation Handling.
Value Proposition:

  • Delivers context-specific recommendations tailored to users’ responsibilities.
  • Enhances operational performance by reducing response times and streamlining decisions.

Capabilities:

  • "My Situations - Extended" app with AI-generated recommendations.
  • Integrates recommendation services with external data via large language models (LLMs).
  • Provides actionable suggestions based on data context and user responsibilities.

Sign up here: Link

AI-Assisted Easy Enterprise Search

AI-assisted easy enterprise search allows you to use natural language through the power of generative AI to search for business objects across the solution by simply typing your search query in the search field of the SAP Fiori launchpad. 

Value Proposition

  • Time saving when searching for known criteria
  • Trustworthy AI results
  • Intuitive, click-efficient user experience, especially for new users
  • Easy to activate on end-user level

Capabilities

  • Setting for activation / de-activation of the natural language search capability on individual end-user level
  • Natural language support of the search field in the SAP Fiori launchpad
  • Indication that natural language search is enabled in the search field itself

Please register to be part of the SAP Beta Testing of the Newest AI-assisted user experience features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Sign up here: Link

 

AI-Assisted Smart Personalization of My Home

AI-assisted smart personalization of My Home allows you to use natural language through the power of generative AI to add insights cards to your My Home experience.

Starting on My Home directly, you can add insights cards using natural language in two clicks including a preview to let the end user review the result generated by AI.

Value Proposition

  • Time saving when adding insights cards
  • Better insights into business data
  • Trustworthy AI results
  • Intuitive, click-efficient user experience, especially for new users

Capabilities

  • One-click action from My Home to trigger the addition of insights cards
  • Support of nine SAP Fiori elements-based apps as basis for insights cards creation during Beta testing
  • Preview during addition of insights cards to ensure data quality and consistency

Please register to be part of the SAP Beta Testing of the Newest AI-assisted user experience features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Sign up here: Link

 

AI-Assisted Error Explanation

AI-assisted error explanation supports in understanding errors in SAP Fiori elements-based applications better and provides you with first recommendations how to solve the errors by leveraging the power of generative AI.

Directly embedded in the current error visualization, it gives you the opportunity to generate explanations only when required.

Value Proposition

  • Time saving when solving errors
  • Intuitively integrated into the current error visualization
  • Better insights into errors and recommended solutions
  • Intuitive, click-efficient user experience, especially for new users

Capabilities

  • One-click action from current error visualization to trigger the explanation
  • Well-structured result of the error explanation containing the explanatory part as well as the recommendations part
  • Possibility to leave sentiment of the feature

Please register to be part of the SAP Beta Testing of the Newest AI-assisted user experience features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Sign up here: Link

 

AI-Assisted Configuration for US Tax Jurisdictions in Finance

This feature leverages AI to automatically extract key information from natural language sentences or Excel files, setting up jurisdiction-based tax configurations with minimal manual input. The AI-assisted configuration for US Tax Jurisdictions reduces the effort required for users to configure tax settings accurately, minimizing manual errors. Benefits include:

  • Accuracy and Efficiency: Reduces manual errors and enhances setup accuracy.
  • Modernized Onboarding: Natural language interactions and an intuitive interface streamline the onboarding process, cutting down both time and costs.

 

4. Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM) for Generative AI Use Cases

 

SAP enhances its AI framework with ISLM, providing a robust structure for managing AI use cases. This feature facilitate adoption of generative AI in SAP S/4HANA by managing SAP-delivered and custom AI use cases within a centralized, end-to-end framework.


Value Proposition:

  • Integrate business applications with large language models, simplifying AI implementation and enabling customers and partners to develop custom use cases for transforming core business processes
  • Provide support for predelivered generative AI-based use cases in RISE with SAP
  • Allow the delivery of integrated ISLM and ABAP AI SDK experience, enabling development of generative AI-based use cases, including custom creation
  • Enable turnkey auto-activation for generative AI-based use cases

Capabilities:

  • Manages AI use cases throughout their lifecycle, from exploration to activation.
  • Supports both narrow AI and generative AI use cases.
  • Offers turnkey auto-activation for rapid implementation of AI solutions.

 

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!   

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience–anywhere, anytime.  

  

🔎Find All of Our Assets   

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Maximizing the Value of AI for HR
entry.content:
Organizations today recognize the potential of AI to improve their business processes and workforce outcomes, but they can only realize AI’s promised value if their workforce understands and effectively adopts new AI tools. To understand the current state of AI at work – and in particular, HR – the SAP SuccessFactors HR research team hosted interviews with 79 HR and IT professionals from 59 SAP SuccessFactors customer organizations and conducted a global survey of 4,023 individual contributors and people managers across regions and industries. We have taken insights from the research and highlighted strategic and technological recommendations for organizations to capitalize on the potential of AI. 

The first report, "HR’s guide to enterprise AI adoption: Strategies for AI governance and workforce engagement," discusses the barriers that organizations face to greater adoption of AI tools, one of them being the fact that 78% of organizations do not have a specific budget for AI investments. We provide recommendations for how to overcome those barriers by developing a comprehensive AI governance model and implementing employee-centered adoption interventions. 

The second report, "HR’s guide to improving AI literacy and AI readiness across the business," centers around the importance of improving AI literacy across the workforce in order to realize business outcomes from AI. In fact, our research shows that people with low AI literacy are between 4.3 and 11.8 times less likely to have used AI at work. This report also discusses the unique needs of employees, managers, and HR professionals when it comes to understanding AI and how to use it. 

To access other content from this research program, visit the Resources page of the HCM section on SAP.com. We hope you find it useful in maximizing the value of AI for HR.

entry.title: Structured output while working with LLMs
entry.content:
Have you ever faced issues with insufficient control over your large language model's output when developing applications?  Often, models are not configured to produce results tailored to their specific needs, leading to outputs that are less useful or relevant. This oversight is a recurring issue in many projects. Developers frequently neglect to design their prompts in a way that guides the model to generate outputs that can be practically applied to their use case. This misstep can result in inefficiencies and additional work to manually adjust or interpret the model’s responses.

One could easily solve this problem by specifying the output format in the prompt. One should be precise what output is required and its structure. Passing the structure of the output helps a lot in such situation.

Langchain addresses this problem by offering a comprehensive suite of tools known as Output Parsers. These tools are specifically designed to help developers manage and format the model's output effectively. Output parsers enable precise control over the structure and content of the responses generated by the language model, ensuring that the outputs align closely with the intended application. By utilizing output parsers, developers can streamline their workflows, reduce errors, and enhance the overall usefulness of their applications. This makes Langchain a powerful resource for anyone looking to harness the full potential of large language models in a controlled and productive manner.

Lets get started with techniques to address this issue effectively:

  1. Structured output without Langchain
  2. Structured output with Langchain (Pydantic parser)

Structured output without Langchain

In this technique, we pass the structure of the output we expect LLM to give. This is simply passed in prompt.

CODE

 

import requests
output_format = {
    "product":"Product mentioned in Paragraph",
    "price_in_rupees":"Price of the product in rupees",
    "price_in_dollar":"Price of the product in dollars",
    "credit_card_number":"Credit card number provided"
}
prompt = f"""You are given a paragraph. You have to extract information from the above given paragraph.\
    Paragraph : I saw Nike's sneakers on amazon. They are for 67 american dollars which is approximately equivalent to \
    5600 Indian rupees. Here is the credit card with number XXXX-ZZZZ-AAAA-IIII.

    Output Format : {output_format}

    Please don't give any analysis from your side. Just return the required result in given format.
    """
url = "<your-endpoint>"
headers = {
    "Authorization": "Bearer <token-here>",
    "Content-Type":"application/json",
    "AI-Resource-Group":"default"
}
payload = {
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": prompt
    }
  ]
}
response = requests.post(f"{url}",
                                     headers=headers,
                                 json=payload)
print("\n\nStructured Output :")
print(response.json()["choices"][0]["message"]["content"])

 

OUTPUT

This is the simplest way one can achieve structured output with any framework. But this approach has one problem, the result is returned in string format. We need to pre-process the data before we use it in code. There can be chance where output can have escape characters or any other unwanted characters which we need to handle manually.

Structured output with Langchain (Pydantic parser)

It's important to remember that large language models are leaky abstractions. This means that they might not always produce perfect results, and the quality of the output can vary depending on the complexity of the task and the capacity of the model being used. To generate well-formed JSON or other structured outputs, it's crucial to use an LLM with sufficient capacity and to design prompts that clearly specify the desired format.

By utilizing output parsers and carefully crafting prompts, developers can significantly improve the reliability and usefulness of the outputs from large language models, making their applications more efficient and effective. This makes Langchain a powerful resource for anyone looking to harness the full potential of large language models in a controlled and productive manner.

Prerequisite:

Create a requirements.txt file 

requirements.txt

 

generative-ai-hub-sdk==4.1.1
langchain==0.3.0
langchain-aws==0.2.1
langchain-community==0.3.0
langchain-core==0.3.28
langchain-google-genai==2.0.1
langchain-google-vertexai==2.0.1
langchain-openai==0.2.9
langchain-text-splitters==0.3.0

 

 

Install the dependencies using below command:

 

pip install -r requirements.txt

 

CODE

 

from gen_ai_hub.proxy import GenAIHubProxyClient
from gen_ai_hub.proxy.langchain.openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain.output_parsers import PydanticOutputParser
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from langchain.prompts import PromptTemplate
from typing import List
gen_ai_hub_proxy_client = GenAIHubProxyClient(
                                    base_url=<AICORE_BASE_URL>,
                                    auth_url=<AICORE_AUTH_URL>,
                                    client_id=<CLIENT_ID>,
                                    client_secret=<CLIENT_SECRET>,
                                    resource_group=<RESOURCE_GROUP>
                                )
chat_model = ChatOpenAI(proxy_client=gen_ai_hub_proxy_client,
                                     proxy_model_name= "gpt-4",
                                     temperature= 0)

class Product(BaseModel):
    products: List[str] = Field(description="Products mentioned in Paragraph", default=[])
    price_nike: int = Field(description="Price mentioned for Nike sneakers", default=0)
    price_LV: float = Field(description="Price mentioned for LV sneakers", default=0.0)
    credit_card_number: str = Field(description="Credit card number provided", default="None")
    shipping_address: str = Field(description="Address for shipment of product", default="7th street, Hill Road")


parser = PydanticOutputParser(pydantic_object=Product)

prompt = PromptTemplate(
    template="You are given a paragraph. You have to extract information from the above given paragraph. If no data is available to extract \
            just return output with default value\n{format_instructions}\n{paragraph}\n",
    input_variables=["paragraph"],
    partial_variables={"format_instructions": parser.get_format_instructions()},
)

paragraph = f"""I saw Nike's sneakers on amazon. They are for $67. I also saw LV's sneakers, they were for $1499.67\
      Here is the credit card with number XXXX-ZZZZ-AAAA-IIII."""
chain = prompt | chat_model | parser

response=chain.invoke({"paragraph": paragraph})
print("Structured Output :")
print(response)

 

OUTPUT

One notable feature of pydantic parsers is the ability to set default values. Paragraph provided to the LLM does not include a shipping address. Despite this, the output can still contain an address by using default values specified in the pydantic model. This is incredibly useful for handling cases where certain information is either not extractable by the LLM or simply not available in the input data. By providing default values, developers can ensure that the output remains complete and usable.

Additionally, pydantic parsers allow for specifying the data type for each key in the output. This ensures that the extracted data is not only present but also in the correct format, reducing the risk of type-related errors downstream in the application. The overall output object is an instance of a pydantic class, "Product" in this case, encapsulating all the specified keys and their corresponding values.

In summary, controlling the output of large language models is critical for developing useful and reliable applications. Langchain's output parsers provide an essential toolset for achieving this control, with features like pydantic models, default values, and data type specifications. By leveraging these tools and understanding the inherent limitations of LLMs, developers can create more robust applications that truly meet their needs. Langchain stands out as a valuable asset for anyone aiming to maximize the potential of large language models in their projects.

 

NOTE : The output varies from model to model. This blog was written considering GPT-4 as a LLM model.

entry.title: SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Silicon Valley highlighted how AI makes app development easier
entry.content:
Thanks to all the local customers who joined us for SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Silicon Valley on February 5, 2025. By the end of the day, everyone saw the latest and greatest AI innovations that guide business users through their SAP tasks more quickly and make developers more productive by automating and accelerating key parts of their work.

For many, the highlight was the hands-on session where they had the opportunity to build an SAP Fiori app, starting with a sketch of what they want the app to look like. They learned about generative AI, the Flexible Programming Model in SAP Fiori elements, and how to fine-tune apps using SAP Fiori tools.

My favorite part of the event were the conversations with customers during the breaks where I had a chance to learn more about their specific industries and the unique challenges they face. This reinforces the need for some level of customization in the starting page, induvial apps, and business processes.

Schedule for SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Silicon Valley

The day started with an overview of SAP Fiori and what this looks like in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Similar to how you would begin your day with SAP, we started with My Home and all the different ways you have to launch your SAP Fiori apps and make the page your own.

Graphic showing personalization options in My Home by creating the cards you want to see

We then showed examples of the SAP Fiori user experience across SAP S/4HANA, including many of the innovations that we have recently added to our public cloud version. We also presented several ways on how to create cards to show key information in My Home.

SAP Fiori includes more AI capabilities for business users

In the morning, we showed all the released AI functionality in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, much of which we introduced in 2502.

  • Easy filter – natural language searching in SAP Fiori elements list reports
  • Smart summarization – AI-created summary of SAP Fiori elements object pages
  • Card creation in My Home– several ways to create a card that you can add to My Home
  • Joule-assisted insight card creation in My Home– ask Joule for the information you need and create a card that you can add to My Home

We ended the day with some previews of planned AI functionality that will first be released into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

  • Recap – a summary of what you missed when you were away from your SAP system
  • Assisted error resolution – providing more context on system errors with recommendations to resolve them
  • Microsoft CoPilot integration

Some of these are currently in beta testing with selected public cloud customers.

Clean core is how you can extend your SAP S/4HANA system.

One important area that many customers are interested in hearing about is how to extend their SAP S/4HANA systems. This could be creating new apps or moving existing customizations from their on-prem environments into their private cloud implementation. There are many options, some on-stack in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system and some on the side in BTP.

Graphic of the options to extend your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system in a clean core way

RISE extensibility guide

GROW extensibility guide

The partners shared their vison for the future and how to get there

Event gold sponsors cbs, Lemongrass, and Qubittron presented about various use cases for AI and how they have helped customers overcome initial hesitation. They also shared their wisdom in a panel discussion that covered different dimensions of UX transformation along with SAP Fiori adoption, change management, and how to gain alignment among different stakeholders in an organization.

Thanks to everyone who made SAP Fiori Innovation Day a success

SAP Presenters (in order of appearance): Sebastian Steinhauer, Katja Zoch, Chad Leonard, Vikas Lodha, Dan Watters, Carolina Robertson, Sushant Priyadarshi, Conrad Bernal, Ashley Tung, Marcel Kassner

Gold partner sponsors: Qubittron, cbs Corporate Business Solutions, lemongrass

Silver partner sponsor: qBotica

Behind the scenes team: all the developers, architects, product managers and other colleagues who built the functionality and set up the demos and hands-on systems.

Without all the great customer interaction, questions, and conversations during meals and breaks, we would not have learned what’s most important to you as you move forward in each of your respective organizations in your UX transformation projects.

 

Next steps

  • Register for the monthly SAP Fiori development roundtable call to meet other developers and architects who build SAP Fiori apps.
  • Subscribe to the SAP Fiori development newsletter, in which we share updates on SAP Fiori elements, SAP Fiori tools, SAPUI5, and SAP Build Code every other month.  
  • Read about last year’s SAP Fiori Innovation Days events in Heidelberg, Chicago, and Dublin.
  • Join us at an SAP Fiori Development Innovation Day in 2025 – we are finalizing the locations and dates.

entry.title: Beyond the Data Silo: The Convergence of SAP Systems and Modern Data Lake Technologies
entry.content:
In today's enterprise landscape, a fascinating transformation is underway. While SAP systems continue to serve as the backbone of business operations, organizations are discovering that traditional approaches to data management no longer meet the demands of our rapidly evolving digital economy. The challenge isn't just about managing data – it's about turning it into actionable intelligence at the speed of business.

The Evolution of SAP Data Architecture

The SAP ecosystem has long been the backbone of enterprise operations, housing critical business data across its various modules. However, today's digital economy demands more than just robust transaction processing – it requires seamless integration of internal and external data sources, real-time analytics, and AI-driven insights.

The current state of enterprise data presents an intriguing paradox. Consider a typical global manufacturing Company X: Multiple SAP instances span continents, each running critical modules like FI/CO, MM, SD, and PP. In Europe, teams work with one set of customizations and configurations, while their Americas counterparts operate with different chart of accounts and business rules. What seems like a simple question about global inventory levels can spiral into a days-long exercise involving multiple teams, countless Excel sheets, and lengthy email threads.

 

Traditional-architecture

These challenges extend beyond just SAP systems. The integration of external data sources – IoT sensors, market data feeds, social media analytics – adds layers of complexity to an already intricate landscape. The time lag between data creation and insight generation has become a critical bottleneck for business agility.

The Promise of Modern Data Lakes

Modern data lake technologies represent a paradigm shift in how enterprises can manage and utilize their data. These platforms bring transformative capabilities through their support for industry-standard formats, enabling seamless data exchange across the enterprise. Their flexible schema management adapts to changing business needs, while enterprise-grade reliability is ensured through ACID transactions. The ability to access and restore historical data states, combined with unified processing for both batch and streaming workloads, creates a robust foundation for next-generation enterprise data management.

 

The Convergence: A New Enterprise Data Architecture

The future lies in architectures that bring together the best of both worlds – SAP's robust business processes and the flexibility of modern data lakes. This next-generation architecture reimagines how enterprise data flows and interacts:

 

Future-architecture

 This convergence enables a new approach to data governance, where metadata management, data quality rules, and lineage tracking are centralized across both SAP and non-SAP data. Organizations can achieve zero-latency access to operational data with instant synchronization across systems, while performing real-time analytics without impacting transaction systems.

Perhaps most importantly, this convergence creates an AI-ready data foundation. By standardizing data formats for AI/ML workloads and enabling the integration of structured and unstructured data, organizations can fully leverage the power of large language models and generative AI across their enterprise data landscape.

Practical Implementation Strategies

Organizations looking to modernize their SAP data architecture should begin with a focus on high-value scenarios that require integrated data, particularly in areas where real-time insights drive business value. Projects that combine SAP and external data often yield the most significant returns and provide valuable learning experiences for teams. The foundation of this modernization requires implementing modern data lake capabilities alongside existing systems, while establishing unified governance frameworks. Creating semantic layers that abstract technical complexity enables broader adoption across the organization. Organizations must also prepare their data structures for AI workloads, implementing robust data quality measures and building pipelines for continuous data refreshes. 

Conclusion: Preparing for Tomorrow

The convergence of SAP systems with modern data lake technologies isn't just a technical evolution – it's a business imperative. Organizations that successfully navigate this transformation will find themselves better positioned to accelerate innovation through integrated data access, improve decision-making with real-time insights, enable AI-driven business processes, and maintain competitive advantage in the digital economy.

The question isn't whether to embrace this transformation, but how to implement it in a way that maximizes business value while minimizing disruption. As technology leaders, our role is to guide our organizations through this evolution, ensuring we build data architectures that are not just modern, but future-ready.

What steps is your organization taking to modernize its enterprise data architecture? The journey toward unified, AI-ready data platforms is just beginning, and the decisions we make today will shape our ability to compete tomorrow.

entry.title: Announcing the Water Track at the SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference 2025
entry.content:
For the third consecutive year, we are happy to announce a dedicated track for water and wastewater companies in the SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference agenda for this year.

It will take place on Wednesday afternoon April 9th with the aim to recognize the success of this section in previous conference editions and continue giving floor to a selection of exceptional professionals not only presenting, but building relationships and developing partnerships among this industry group.

Shifting from an agenda designed last year to show the concept architecture built by SAP to support water management processes to help organizations leveraging their software investments to tackle processes such as smart meter data management, named the “Smart Platform for Water”, current year be focused on cases where SAP portfolio of applications and technologies can help organizations confront their challenges and obtain operational benefits.

Mrs Inge Opreel, Farys CIO

I am delighted to inform you about the presentions of the Water Track:

  • SAP for Water and Wastewater Services: Building the Resilient Water Company (SAP Keynote)
  • ESRI ArcGIS on HANA: Building Next Generation of Integrated Asset Management at FARYS
  • Streaming Service Operations: SAP Field Service Management (FSM) Integration at Vitens

But the agenda of water-specific items will not be restricted to this track but will be highly recommended water-specific or water-relevant conferences before and after the Water Track which will configure an entire agenda specifically for delegates of this industry:

SAP Business Data Cloud

Wednesday morning, April 9th (before the Water Track): suggested slots unveil the large ongoing updates in the portfolio of Asset Management, including an interesting use case of Asset Performance Management as well as an excellent presentation by SAP Product Development of the evolution of SAP analytical  portfolio, this is Business Data Cloud:   

  • Latest Innovations in SAP Asset Management (Asset Management track)
  • Business Data Cloud – Unlocking the Power of Unified Data
  • SAP Asset Performance Management (APM) at AkerBP:  From Test Environment to Production

Thursday morning, April 10th will be a continuation of the water-specific cases, presenting the application of AI with SAP technology platform at FARYS, a live demonstration at the Pop-up Campus (which will require a pre-booking)

 

 

 

Inside of SAP Pop-Up Campus

A very interesting use case of RISE conversion, from a perspective very close to the reality of many water companies'.

  • Unlocking the Potential of AI with SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and Datasphere (SAP Microforum at 10:30AM 
  • A Day in a Life of a Water Drop: Deep Dive into the Benefits of Asset Management at the Lifecycle of a Waterdrop (session needs to be pre-booked via the app)
  • Transformation from OnPrem to SAP RISE: Lessons from Andel’s Journey (AI Track)

Overall, the complete conference is designed for an era based in networks, relationships and reputation, where collaboration and developing partnerships is the key of success. But it is known that such relationships cannot be automated and the soul of this track, and the overall water community formation, is the inheritance of the Executive Value Network for Water and Waste Water Companies workshops. This is a regular meeting of water companies taking place in Europe (already six editions), Latin America (three) and now Africa. Participants highlight common challenges and go along with case studies of how SAP customers are leveraging SAP solutions to address them. Take this unique opportunity to connect with your peers, gain insights into industry trends, and chart your development plan with the latest technologies of 2025!

 Looking forward to seeing you in Rotterdam!

entry.title: AI@FRE (Part 5) - Guardrail Enterprise AI Adoption – AI for People and Team
entry.content:
### AI@FRE Blog Series:

AI@FRE (Part 1) - Enabling Enterprise AI Adoption through the BTP FRE Engagement Model
AI@FRE (Part 2) - Accelerating Business AI Transformation with the FRE Value Framework
AI@FRE (Part 3) – Guardrails for Enterprise AI Adoption
AI@FRE (Part 4) - Guardrail Enterprise AI Adoption – AI Architecture Guardrails
AI@FRE (Part 5) - Guardrail Enterprise AI Adoption – AI for People and Team
AI@FRE (PART 6) - Guardrail Enterprise AI Adoption – Establish AI Ethics, Security & Privacy

###

Adopting AI in the enterprise goes beyond implementing technology; it requires a people-centered approach. Organizations that successfully integrate AI empower their teams with new skills, redefine roles, and foster a collaborative environment where AI becomes a strategic enabler rather than a disruptor. This blog explores two essential steps for AI adoption: embracing AI within the organization and building robust, AI-capable teams. 

Embracing and Onboarding AI in the Workplace 

For AI to become part of the enterprise fabric, employees need to feel supported, informed, and engaged. Moving AI from a novelty to a standardized tool requires careful alignment with organizational goals and a focus on continuous learning. 

Define the Challenge 

Many employees are apprehensive about AI, often viewing it as complex or a potential threat to job security. Overcoming these perceptions involves: 

  • Effective Communication and Training: Employees must understand how AI can assist rather than replace them. 
  • Empowering Employees: Provide tools and support to integrate AI into everyday tasks and processes. 
  • Cultivating a Culture of Innovation: Encourage experimentation, learning from mistakes, and collaborative exploration of AI’s potential across roles. 

Approach 

  • Communicate and Align: Clearly communicate AI’s capabilities and align them with organizational goals. This ensures employees understand how AI aligns with the company's vision and how it will enhance their roles. 
  • Identify Use Cases: Identify specific business challenges where AI can make a difference, such as automating repetitive tasks or enabling faster, data-driven decisions. 
  • Pilot Projects: Start small by implementing pilot projects that demonstrate AI’s impact on real tasks. These early successes help build confidence and support for further AI initiatives. 
  • Governance and Ethics: Develop a framework to ensure AI is used responsibly. Considerations around data privacy, fairness, and ethical use are paramount to building trust. 
  • Continuous Improvement: Learn from each AI project to mature AI adoption. Celebrate successes, iterate on learnings, and keep refining AI implementations for improved performance and adoption. 

Outcomes and Follow-Up Actions 

  • Invest in Training and Change Management: Ongoing training is critical to help employees gain new skills and fully understand the role of AI in their jobs. 
  • Augment Human Skills: Use AI to optimize and automate repetitive, tiring tasks, allowing employees to focus on more creative, strategic responsibilities. 
  • Opportunity to Redefine Jobs: With AI handling routine tasks, employees can redefine their roles to work alongside AI, focusing on higher-level problem-solving and innovative projects. 

Building AI Teams, Enhancing Capabilities, and Complementing Skill Sets 

Building a capable AI team requires hiring talent, upskilling existing employees, and integrating AI expertise across departments. By assembling an AI-proficient team, organizations create the foundation needed for strategic AI deployment and continuous improvement. 

Define the Challenge 

Organizations face challenges when trying to integrate AI into existing workflows and business processes. A successful AI team should: 

  • Address Skill Gaps: Existing teams may lack AI expertise, necessitating new hires or targeted training. 
  • Facilitate Seamless AI Integration: AI should align smoothly with various business functions, avoiding disruptions. 
  • Foster a Collaborative Environment: Effective AI adoption requires cross-functional cooperation to leverage AI’s full potential. 

Approach 

  • Talent Acquisition: Recruit professionals with expertise in AI, machine learning, data science, and related fields. Skilled hires strengthen the core AI team, bringing fresh perspectives and knowledge. 
  • Training and Development: Offer training programs for existing employees to enhance AI skills, complementing their current skill sets and fostering adaptability. 
  • Cross-functional Collaboration: Promote collaboration between AI experts and other departments. By pairing domain experts with AI specialists, organizations can drive innovation that’s informed by practical, hands-on knowledge. 
  • Continuous Learning: Encourage a culture of ongoing learning to keep the team updated on AI advancements, emerging tools, and best practices. 
  • Trusted Partnerships and External Collaborations: Establish partnerships with institutions, industry experts, and other AI-specialized organizations to supplement internal expertise, bringing in specialized insights and resources. 

 

Outcomes and Follow-Up Actions 

  • Define an AI Strategy: A proficient team can actively shape the organization’s AI strategy and uphold AI ethics, ensuring responsible AI usage. 
  • Train, Explain, and Sustain AI: The team should be skilled at training AI systems, explaining outcomes to stakeholders, and ensuring continuous monitoring for proper functionality. 
  • Augment AI Skills for Scalability: As the organization scales AI, standardized processes and tools become vital, and an empowered AI team will be prepared to lead these efforts, supporting the wider organization in adopting AI more systematically. 

 

Building a Sustainable AI Culture for the Future 

Creating a sustainable AI culture is an ongoing journey. It begins by fostering AI adoption at the individual and team levels, equipping employees with the tools and training they need to integrate AI into daily operations. AI-powered organizations focus on: 

  1. Empowering Teams: Use AI to relieve employees of repetitive tasks, enabling them to focus on impactful, creative work. 
  1. Continuous Skill Enhancement: Organizations should prioritize training and development to stay at the forefront of AI innovation. 
  1. Purpose-Driven AI Projects: AI should support clear, purpose-driven goals, demonstrating tangible business value and improving organizational agility. 

 

An organization with AI-empowered employees and cross-functional AI teams is better equipped to adapt to market changes, make data-driven decisions, and continuously innovate. By investing in people-first AI adoption, businesses can not only enhance operational efficiency but also create an environment where AI is a valued, collaborative tool for every team member. 

 

Curious on a structured customer engagement model to set up standardized Cloud Architecture for Business AI Adoption? Connect with our Global BTP Customer Engagement Team at BTPFRE@sap.com.

entry.title: Leveraging API Traffic Predictions in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Introduction

In today's fast-paced digital landscape, efficiently managing API traffic and predicting future trends are crucial for maintaining seamless operations. SAP Integration Suite introduces a powerful feature called Predictions, which leverages AI to forecast future API call volumes based on historical data. This blog post will guide you through understanding, enabling, and viewing predictions, helping you optimize your API strategy and make informed decisions.

 

Key Use Cases

Traffic Predictions:

    • As an API Owner, you can see the API traffic prediction for all your deployed APIs for any duration in the next three months. They can further be aggregated to see the data day-wise, week-wise or month-wise.
    • Forecasting the API traffic during a specific time period enables planning ahead on resource allocation, optimizing costs, improving performance, reliability, and scalability. For example, a travel booking site might predict increased API traffic during holiday seasons. This foresight allows the site to scale up its infrastructure in advance, ensuring it can handle the higher demand without performance issues.

Weekly Trends:

    • As an API Owner, you can see the typical weekly trend of API traffic in your tenant.
    • Weekly trends help identify API traffic patterns that occur within a week, aiding decision-making. For example, an e-commerce platform might notice higher API traffic on weekends due to increased shopping activity. This insight allows the platform to allocate more resources during weekends to handle the surge in traffic and ensure a smooth user experience.

Daily Trends:

    • As an API Owner, you can see the typical daily trend of API traffic in your tenant.
    • Daily trends help identify API traffic patterns during a 24-hour period and enable decision-making. For instance, a food delivery service might observe peak API traffic during lunch and dinner hours. By identifying these peak hours, the service can avoid rolling out updates during these times to minimize disruption and ensure timely deliveries.

 

How does it work?  

The Predictions feature in SAP Integration Suite analyzes past API call data to forecast future traffic volumes. By identifying trends, it provides insights into expected traffic patterns, helping API owners make informed decisions.

 

Enabling Predictions

As an Administrator of a specific Integration Suite - API Management tenant, one could decide to turn the feature on from the Settings -> APIs page of the Integration Suite application with a simple toggle switch. The toggle button activates both Anomaly Detection and Predictions for API calls.

Note: The feature is in the process of getting updated in our global Data centres. Check this Note for information about regional availability.

Note: The availability of the feature is dependent on your SAP Integration Suite service plan. Check this Note for information about the various plans and supported features.

 

Viewing Predictions and Traffic Trends

Once enabled, you can view API call predictions and trends in the analytics dashboard under a separate tab called Predictions. It offers a visualization of expected traffic patterns over a specified timeframe. You can customize the duration and frequency of predictions to suit your needs.

 

Key Features

  • API Call Predictions: Analyze future API call traffic for specific timeframes such as daily, weekly, or monthly.
  • API Call Trends: Examine the frequency of API calls within set timeframes, such as hourly or daily.
  • Customizable Options: Select specific API proxies, choose the prediction duration, and set the frequency according to your preferences.

 

Benefits of Using Predictions

By leveraging the Predictions feature, you can:

  • Optimize Resource Allocation: Ensure your resources are allocated efficiently based on expected traffic.
  • Manage System Load: Anticipate high traffic periods and prepare your systems accordingly.
  • Make Informed Decisions: Use insights from traffic trends to refine your API strategy and improve overall performance.

 

Conclusion

The Predictions feature in SAP Integration Suite is a valuable tool for any organization looking to enhance its API management capabilities. By forecasting future API call volumes and identifying traffic trends, you can optimize resource allocation, manage system load, and make informed decisions to drive your business forward.

As always, we look forward to hearing your feedback!

entry.title: AI-assisted process modeling: instantly transform text into BPMN diagrams
entry.content:
Transforming the future of business process management 

The landscape of process modeling is evolving rapidly. As the demand for documenting and analyzing business processes increases, process modelers face growing challenges in keeping up with the pace. Limited capacity, uncertainty on where to start, and the need for more efficient collaboration between process experts and BPMN specialists often slow down the process. 

What if you could begin with a draft and refine it instead of starting from scratch? What if business experts, rather than only BPMN specialists, could contribute directly to process models? These are the questions driving SAP Signavio’s upcoming innovation: AI-assisted process modeler, text to process (see availability and activation).  

Introducing AI-assisted process modeler, text to process in SAP Signavio 

As the first feature under our AI-assisted process modeler initiative, text to process enables process modelers and subject matter experts to significantly reduce the time required to create new processes. By simply providing a text prompt, AI automatically translates it into a well-structured BPMN diagram, which can then be further refined and customized. 

Check it out with this video.

How it works 

Imagine you have just concluded a workshop with colleagues, capturing key process steps in a whiteboarding tool, a PDF document, or a Word file. Instead of manually converting this information into a BPMN model, you can now input the text directly into SAP Signavio, and AI will generate an initial process model for you. If needed, you can regenerate the model from scratch or fine-tune the generated diagram. 

What makes a good prompt? 

To ensure the best possible BPMN diagram generation, a good prompt should follow these best practices:  

  • Define the process scope 
    • Clearly state the objective of the process. 
    • Identify start and end points, along with any key milestones.  
  • Structure the process logically  
    • Describe a Start Event to indicate where the process begins.  
    • Break the process into clear activities or tasks.  
    • Use a sequential order that mirrors real-world execution.  
  • Include decision points and conditions  
    • Specify conditions that determine different pathways.  
    • Define actions based on different outcomes (e.g., approval vs. rejection scenarios).  
  • Ensure accuracy and completeness  
    • Provide sufficient detail while avoiding unnecessary complexity.  
    • Make sure key actors, dependencies, and interactions are clearly stated 

Example of a good prompt: 

"The process starts when a payroll data alert is assigned. The agent assesses the alert. If no data change is needed, the agent comments and closes the alert. If a data change is required, the agent consults with the line manager, determines if an update is needed, and corrects the payroll data if necessary. If additional updates are required, the agent works with the system administrator to finalize changes before closing the alert." 

BPMN diagram output 

 

 Benefits 

Process modeling for an enterprise can cost hundreds of thousands of dollars annually, factoring in the time it takes to collect inputs, create or update hundreds of process models. By leveraging the AI-assisted process modeler, text to process capability, this amount can be reduced significantly while giving time back to then be used to customers advantage. 

Key benefits include: 

  • Faster time to value: Rapidly generate process models from text, reducing the time spent on manual diagram creation. 
  • Enhanced collaboration: Simplify complex process descriptions by turning them into visual models, improving cross-team communication. 
  • Flexible editing: Ensure continuous improvement of models with the ability to save and refine them in SAP Signavio Process Manager. 

Availability and activation 

The AI-assisted process modeler, text to process feature is planned to be generally available in March 2025 (subject to change). Reach out to your account executive or customer success partner for updates on release date and more information on how to access this capability. Also visit the roadmap explorer for further details.    

The future of AI in process modeling 

This is just the beginning of an exciting journey towards AI-driven process modeling. In the coming months, SAP Signavio will continue to introduce innovative AI-powered features, enhancing productivity and enabling more intuitive process management. 

Stay tuned for more updates and start transforming the way you model business processes with AI!

entry.title: 11 great Business AI prototypes at the Capgemini private Hack2Build!
entry.content:
Introduction

The Hack2Build initiative is traditionally a 7-day challenge for multiple partners to build working prototypes. At first each partner's submitted use case is evaluated for technical feasibility and customer relevance by SAP. Then SAP experts provide guidance and coaching to partners with accepted use cases. Finally partners are invited to demo their prototype to a panel of judges in a demo jam. 

Our partner Capgemini is obviously taking Business AI very seriously and approached us shortly after SAP Sapphire 2024 with 40+ Business AI use cases across SAP business portfolio, prepared by various teams around the world. A first review showed the potential so we jointly decided to kick off the Capgemini private Hack2Build: an initiative to help Capgemini develop Business AI solutions that are relevant, reliable, and responsible for the 10-15 most promising use cases.

About the Capgemini private Hack2Build Initiative

Capgemini used SAP AI Core along with SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) services, including SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine's Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG). These capabilities helped contextualize information with any of the available 23 Large Language Models (LLMs) to provide enterprise-grade responses. Capgemini teams also utilised existing business services to enhance their AI solutions.

The initiative ran from November 19-27, 2024 in an hybrid mode with a few teams working out of the SAPLabs France in Mougins with a few SAP experts together while other teams connected remotely from around the world with the entire SAP team.

The event concluded with a demo jam where Capgemini teams showcased their working prototypes that integrated SAP BTP and SAP AI Core services with both SAP and non-SAP data. The demo jam session produced a winner and two runners-up. This blog highlights the use cases developed by the 3 winning teams as well as the other 8 finalists.

 

Kitchen captAIn
GDPR CompliBuddy
Material Master Data Maintenance
Intelligent Capacity Scheduling
Accelerated GxP Compliance Documentation
Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Modelling,Planning
Emergency Response Simulator
SAP CX - Hyper personalization
GenAI-powered vendor invoice management
Augmented field worker
Intelligent Contract Management

Winning Solutions

The top 3 solutions selected by the panel of judges were:

🥇  Augmented Field Worker
🥈  Accelerated GxP Compliance
🥉  Intelligent Material Master Data Maintenance

 

🥇  Augmented Field Worker

The solution leverages Generative AI, SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), and HANA Database with Vector Engine to automate Non-Conformity (NC) qualification and resolution. It empowers field engineers with a mobile app providing instant similarity analysis based on a picture from a suspected NC, voice-activated commands e.g. with access to necessary documentation to declare an NC, and real-time repair recommendations, streamlining workflows, reducing manual effort, and enabling faster, more accurate decision-making in challenging field environments.

 

🥈  Accelerated GxP Compliance

Accelerated Good Practices (GxP) Compliance Tool provides GenAI-powered pharma documentation capabilities by connecting with core SAP processes via APIs to extract key data points. GenAI drafts initial content based on pre-defined templates and automates data entry based on pharma guidelines and company baselines, ensuring compliance to regulatory bodies and GxP norms.

 

🥉  Intelligent Material Master Data Maintenance

Intelligent Material Master Data Maintenance is a RAG-based SAP solution that helps to reduce of manual workload and errors, supports faster onboarding of materials using AI, increases data accuracy and reduces risks and costs in the supply chain.

 

Finalists

Below a short description of the 8 finalist solutions in no particular order

Kitchen captAIn

Kitchen captAIn is an intelligent solution catering to grocery-based platforms. It provides grocery/product details to customers with specific recipe/menu ideas, highlights details like allergy ans calorie details based on preferences to ensure an optimal and personalized buying experience while maximizing grocery utilization and minimizing waste.

GenAI Hyper personalization

 GenAI Hyperonalization collects real-time clickstream data from website visitors, including country, locations, cart items, purchases, duration, etc. It utilizes the captured data to create dynamic user personas for each session. Business users can generate personalized content and images for the products and delivering instant, tailored content directly on the store front.

Intelligent Contract Management

Intelligent Contract Management provides GenAI-powered contract management capabilities. It connects SAP Ariba processes with APIs to extract key data point, create and review draft contracts with intelligent insights from Ariba and S4HANA, and augment Ariba's Clauses library with new clauses.

Smart Field Report

Smart Field Report addresses the challenges of manual, time-consuming reports generation in Oil & Gas upstream operations e.g. production, drilling, emissions, by automating data extraction, analysis, and reporting on SAP and non-SAP relevant data. It improves efficiency by eliminating human errors, provides accurate and consistent reports, and offers actionable insights for better decision-/recommendations-making. The solution also enables real-time operations, ensures compliance, and drives digital transformation, promoting smarter and data-driven operations.

 

Intelligent Capacity Scheduling

Intelligent Capacity Scheduling tackles production scheduling challenges by using Generative AI to create a dynamic and intelligent scheduling system. It enhances SAP current setup by making schedules more adaptive and data-driven.

 

Panel of Judges

We extend our sincere thanks to the judges who lent their expertise to this initiative. Senior leaders and experts from SAP Business AI, SAP BTP, and Partner Ecosystem Success carefully evaluated 6-minute pitches from participating teams. They assessed each solution’s potential to address real-world challenges using SAP Business AI and provided thoughtful feedback to help partners further refine their solutions in the next phase of Hack2Build.

Helen Kushakovska

SAPLabs Mougins Managing Director

Christian Koenig

Director - Business AI Partner Product Management

Gianluigi Bagnoli

Global VP, BTP Solution Architects, PES

Rudi Held

Vice-President, Global Head of SAP Co-Innovation Lab, PES

Special Thanks

We extend our appreciation to the SAP team members from SAP Business AI, BTP Architecture, Strategic Partner Enablement, and SAP Co-Innovation Lab, whose expertise guided partners to successfully develop their solutions during the Capgemini Private Hack2Build Business AI initiative. Special thanks as well to the entire SAPLabs France team in Mougins for hosting us in their nice facilities.

Way Forward

Hack2Build consists of two phases: the hack phase and the build phase. All finalist teams in this initiative continue working with SAP, maintaining momentum in this co-innovation journey. SAP engages with Capgemini to expand the business AI prototypes into comprehensive offerings for customers.

As of today 3 solutions already made it (below) and 6 are in the process:

Augmented field workerValidated Partner Use Case (VPUC)
Smart Field ReportValidated Partner Use Case (VPUC)
Kitchen captAIn

Validated Partner Use Case (VPUC)

Video

Please click on the VIDEO LINK  for a first impression how the initiative looked like.

 

SAP AI Core SAP Business Technology Platform

entry.title: Deliver AI value across your business with DataRobot Insight Apps for SAP
entry.content:
At DataRobot, we’ve worked with hundreds of clients across industries and one thing remains crystal clear: delivering AI to where users need it, and where they’ll actually use it, is still a challenge. As organizations work to fit AI into their business processes, they’re still running into the same big hurdles. 

Organizations struggle with unifying data and getting it AI-ready, and they struggle with building AI solutions that solve pressing business problems, and delivering those solutions to users in time to make a real impact. Instead of focusing on what needs to be delivered, they get bogged down with making data accessible, creating the infrastructure for AI deployment, and designing solutions.

With the launch of SAP Business Data Cloud, and the AI-powered Insight Apps for SAP from DataRobot, it’s never been easier to move past infrastructure constraints, and focus on delivering real business value from AI. 

This joint solution provides the fastest way to unify data access, operationalize AI, and deliver AI where it’s needed – transforming how organizations work with AI.

Enhancing SAP Business Data Cloud with Insight Apps 

SAP Business Data Cloud unifies data and analytics with companies’ business processes, empowering leaders with complete context for faster decision making and better business planning. This serves as a trusted data foundation, delivering seamless access to reliable, business-ready data and empowering organizations to prepare data for AI and drive innovation with confidence. 

To help organizations leverage this unified data and rapidly deliver relevant and impactful AI solutions, DataRobot is launching AI-powered Insight Apps for Business Data Cloud. 

These new application suites combine deep industry domain experience and best practices with pre-built and customizable solutions that accelerate use cases for planning, enterprise analytics, advanced analytical apps, and predictive forecasting.
